location: Dropped by an Alabaster Lord enemy at the far southern end of the Lake of Rot (beneath Liurnia), reached by riding the rising/sinking stone pillars across the scarlet-rot lake and then climbing the cliff just west of the Grand Cloister entrance to a gravitational anomaly where the Lord stands.
tactics: Cross the Lake of Rot via the timed rising/falling stone pillars, then climb the cliff west of the Grand Cloister entrance to reach the Lord.
Some players report the sword failing to drop if the Lord's death and the player's own near-death happen in the same instant — avoid trading a killing blow while at critical HP, just to be safe.
A second Alabaster Lord dropping the same sword exists in Yelough Anix Tunnel (Consecrated Snowfield), in a pit past the frozen elevator, as a backup if you miss this one.
Scarlet Rot buildup from wading through the lake is dangerous — use rot-negating gear or cross quickly.
lore: Forged from pale, blue-white meteoric ore, this greatsword hides gravity-manipulating magic in its blade. It once belonged to a race of stone-skinned ancients said to have been given life when the meteor that provided the ore struck the earth.

builds.name: Intelligence/Strength Hybrid
builds.why: D-rank scaling in both Strength and Intelligence plus 38 innate magic attack power let it work as a melee weapon that also hits reasonably hard for an INT-leaning build.

builds.name: Gravity-Magic Novelty Build
builds.why: Deals bonus damage against Gravity-affiliated enemies, making it a thematic pairing for a gravity-sorcery-focused Intelligence character.
